location.formative: Denver, CO — University of Northern Colorado; Boston, MA — Berklee College of Music
location.career: Hoboken, NJ (NYC downtown scene) → Seattle, WA (Fremont/Queen Anne, 1989-present)

examples: In Line (1983, ECM) — solo guitar with compound-interval voicings
Lookout for Hope (1988, ECM) — trio voicings omitting 3rds and 5ths
Throughout from Harmony (2019, Blue Note) — implied polyphony across registral space
theory_nodes.description: Voicings with intervals of a tenth or wider between adjacent notes, creating spaciousness through registral distribution. Chord is implied rather than stated. Inverts Jones's dense big-band voicings while serving the same architectural function — constrained resources producing harmonic complexity.

genealogy.relationship: inversion — density replaced by spaciousness, same Amiga Principle

examples: Throughout (Harmony, 2019) — tonal center without tonal identity
Lookout for Hope (1988) — parallel mode common tones emphasized
Paul Motian Trio recordings (1984-2011) — rubato phrasing over suspended harmony
theory_nodes.description: Deliberate avoidance of the major/minor binary by emphasizing modal common tones (root, 4th, 5th) while treating the 3rd and 6th as passing colors. Creates a tonal gravity that listeners feel without being able to name. Extends Miles Davis's modal jazz into a harmonic palette of ambiguity itself.

genealogy.relationship: departure — Frisell moves away from functional progression toward suspended tonality

examples: Solo performances (NPR Tiny Desk, 2019) — two-part counterpoint from single performer
Shenandoah from Guitar in the Space Age! (2014) — delayed echo as second voice
Music IS (2018, OKeh) — layered delay trails as harmonic texture
theory_nodes.description: Delay used not as effect but as compositional technique. The delayed signal operates as a second voice, creating genuine two-part counterpoint from a single performer. When delay time is musically meaningful (dotted quarter, half note), the interaction produces rhythmic patterns neither signal alone contains.

genealogy.relationship: technological extension — Jones achieved counterpoint through section writing; Frisell achieves it through signal processing

examples: Paul Motian Trio recordings — silence carries equal weight to sounded notes
In Line (1983) — solo passages where space is the primary material
Rambler (1985, ECM) — phrases separated by structural silence
theory_nodes.description: Space between notes as a building block, as present and deliberate as any sounded note. Not bebop punctuation but architectural silence — waiting as attention, stillness as action. Jim Hall lineage: fewer notes = more weight per note. Connects to the great blue heron parallel in the S36 vision.

genealogy.relationship: new concept — first instance in the S36 engine

examples: Lookout for Hope (1988) — melodic half-step neighbor tones creating shimmer
Strange Meeting (1987, Nonesuch) — upper-register instability through chromatic approach
Harmony (2019) — chromatic voice leading in the melody over open voicings
theory_nodes.description: Chromatic neighbor tones in the upper register — notes a half-step above or below the target, approached and departed by step — creating shimmering instability. Where Jones's chromaticism operated in the bass register (propelling forward), Frisell's operates in the upper register (suspending in place). Same harmonic material, opposite architectural intent.

genealogy.relationship: registral inversion — bass propulsion becomes upper-register suspension

examples: Solo performances — loop pedal building multiple independent voices in real time
A Change Is Gonna Come (solo guitar arrangement) — three-voice counterpoint via loops
Music IS (2018, OKeh) — extended loop compositions
theory_nodes.description: Loop pedals creating genuine counterpoint: multiple independent melodic and harmonic lines from a single performer. Raises Bach-era contrapuntal questions in real time: voice interaction, resolution against repeating patterns, independence vs. convergence. The Amiga Principle at its most literal — one performer, technology multiplying available voices.

genealogy.relationship: radical reduction — 15-piece ensemble complexity reduced to one performer plus technology

seattle_connection: Fremont/Queen Anne (1989-present). Co-founder with Wayne Horvitz of Seattle's improvised music community. Reversed the talent-export pattern: globally cultivated, chose local settlement.

theory_genealogy: Open voicings — second instance (inversion of degree 0's density)
Modal ambiguity — first instance, new concept
Delay as counterpoint — first instance, technology-as-composition
Silence as structure — first instance, connects to heron parallel
Chromaticism — second instance (upper-register inversion of degree 0's bass-register chromaticism)
Counterpoint through loops — first instance, Amiga Principle in minimal form
immediate_items: Wayne Horvitz (degree 2) inherits: Seattle improvised music community co-founding, shared NYC downtown migration, Pigpen/Zony Mash collaboration details, keyboard-guitar harmonic dialogue
The jazz-to-Americana bridge Frisell built should be traced forward through every subsequent artist who works across genre boundaries
The reversed export pattern (global cultivation → local settlement) should be tracked alongside the original export pattern (local cultivation → global departure) as a second S36 cultural pattern
ECM Records aesthetic (space, silence, meticulous engineering) should be tracked as a production-philosophy thread alongside the production techniques from degree 0
pattern_items: Signal chain as instrument: track how subsequent artists treat amplification and processing as compositional tools vs. decorative effects
The Berklee pipeline appears at both degree 0 (Jones, 1951) and degree 1 (Frisell, 1975) — track all Berklee connections across the 360 degrees

Genre-bridging evolution: Jones (jazz→pop/R&B/film, 1950s-80s) → Frisell (jazz→Americana/folk/country, 1990s-present) — trace how each subsequent artist extends the territory of what jazz harmony can inhabit
The Jim Hall teaching lineage through Frisell to Julian Lage represents a three-generation pedagogical chain — track these chains across the engine

key_albums.title: In Line
key_albums.label: ECM
key_albums.significance: First leader recording; established the open-voicing, space-as-structure approach

key_albums.title: Rambler
key_albums.label: ECM
key_albums.significance: First band recording on ECM; introduced the Frisell ensemble sound

key_albums.title: Lookout for Hope
key_albums.label: ECM
key_albums.significance: Mature ECM-era statement; Hank Roberts, Kermit Driscoll, Joey Baron

key_albums.title: Have a Little Faith
key_albums.label: Nonesuch
key_albums.significance: Americana manifesto; Copland to Muddy Waters on one album

key_albums.title: Nashville
key_albums.label: Nonesuch
key_albums.significance: Country-jazz synthesis with Nashville session musicians

key_albums.title: Guitar in the Space Age!
key_albums.label: OKeh/Sony
key_albums.significance: Surf-rock reconnection; childhood influences filtered through mature technique

key_albums.title: Harmony
key_albums.label: Blue Note
key_albums.significance: Chamber-music culmination; consonance as destination after decades of dissonance
